findings.title: SHADOW-EARTH-053 — China-Aligned Espionage Campaign Exploiting N-Day Microsoft Exchange + IIS Across Asia and Poland
findings.affected_technology: Microsoft Exchange Server (ProxyLogon-class chain), Microsoft IIS, Linux servers via React2Shell
findings.summary: Trend Micro disclosed China-aligned cluster SHADOW-EARTH-053 running active N-day exploitation of unpatched Microsoft Exchange/IIS plus CVE-2025-55182 across Asian governments and Poland, dropping Godzilla web shells and ShadowPad implants.

findings.title: BlackFile Vishing Campaign — Attribution to Cordial Spider + Snarky Spider Confirmed; Full AiTM SSO Mechanics Now Public
findings.affected_technology: Google Workspace, Microsoft SharePoint, HubSpot, Salesforce — accessed via compromised IdP/SSO credentials and AiTM-bypassed MFA
findings.summary: BlackFile (2026-04-26) attributed today to Cordial Spider (UNC6671) and Snarky Spider (UNC6661) within The Com; full AiTM-MFA-bypass mechanic plus notification-suppression-via-inbox-rules now public — harden SSO with FIDO2, alert on MFA-device rotation.

noted.summary: PoC repo count went from ≥6 yesterday to 168 today including five fresh implementations updated within the last hour (Rust, C, Python, Zig, Go) and defensive tooling (BPF LSM blocker, Ansible remediation) — out of remote-services scope but every Linux server / CI runner / jump host on a kernel since 4.14 (Aug 2017) is in scope; patched in 5.10.254+, 5.15.204+, 6.1.170+, 6.6.137+.

Today's BleepingComputer follow-up disclosed an additional technique not in earlier reporting: the worm reads /proc/<pid>/maps + /proc/<pid>/mem of GitHub Actions Runner.Worker to extract every key=value secret directly from runner memory, bypassing log-masking — track this read-pattern as a worm IOC on CI runners.

Disclosed 2026-05-01; no scope, no attribution, no root cause yet — universities and K-12 districts using Canvas should pre-position log collection. Will promote to a full entry once technical detail is published.
Trend Micro reports nearly half of Malaysian, Sri Lankan, and Myanmar SHADOW-EARTH-053 victims also showed SHADOW-EARTH-054 implants — treat as the same threat aperture for IR purposes.

SecurityWeek 2026-05-01 reported a sophisticated Python-based backdoor deploying persistent Windows espionage implants; full advisory text was 403 — track for vendor advisories or YARA rules in the next 48h.

noted.product: AccountDumpling — 30,000 Facebook accounts via Google AppSheet phishing
noted.summary: Vietnamese-linked phishing infrastructure abused Google AppSheet, Netlify, Vercel, and Google Drive to harvest credentials, MFA codes, and ID photos — out of corporate-infra scope, brief anti-phishing detection if your SOC tracks consumer-side brand abuse.
Pre-auth RCE via Host: header stack overflow (CVSS 9.3); PoC public, no vendor patch — niche consumer router population, relevant only if Totolink is in your fleet.
Two additional Totolink consumer router CVEs published 2026-05-01 (CVSS 10.0 / 9.0); same niche population as CVE-2026-7546.
Authentication flaw (CVSS 9.8); not a top-tier plugin by install base but worth checking your WordPress plugin inventory.
Critical flaw (CVSS 9.8) in a niche Chinese PHP framework with very small deployment footprint outside its origin region.

noted.product: Branch Monkey MCP / Astro MCP servers
noted.summary: Two more MCP-server CVEs published 2026-05-01 (CVSS 7.5 / 6.5), continuing the MCP-server-as-attack-surface pattern (mcp-atlassian, nginx-ui, OpenHarness, chatgpt-mcp-server) — add MCP server inventory to internal AppSec tracking.
Operational/judicial item: minor arrested for selling data from the French administrative agency; brief reference, no defensive action.
Two former cybersecurity incident-response professionals sentenced to 4 years for ALPHV ransomware deployment 2023; useful for IR-program insider-risk policy work.
